mechanism_narrative: A rise in the housing market, as indicated by a surge in home sales (HOUST), signals an increase in aggregate demand that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in housing market expansions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate, as indicated by a rise in jobless claims (UNRATE), signals a weakening labor market that depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in labor market contractions are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production, as indicated by a drop in the manufacturing sector (INDPRO), signals a decrease in aggregate supply that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in supply-side contractions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the consumer price index, as indicated by a surge in inflation (CPIAUCSL), signals an increase in aggregate demand that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in inflationary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate, as indicated by a drop in FEDFUNDS, signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the employment rate, as indicated by a drop in jobless claims (PAYEMS), signals an improvement in the labor market that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in labor market recoveries are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the retail sales, as indicated by a surge in RSAFS, signals an increase in aggregate demand that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in retail sales expansions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production, as indicated by a drop in the manufacturing sector (INDPRO), signals a decrease in aggregate supply that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in supply-side contractions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
